scenarios.criterion: On a tick against a project store carrying a prose intent that resolves to no referent, go advances a READY unscoped survey protocol and produces a validated requirements artifact through the methodology output tool, with no work-unit id supplied.
scenarios.given: A runa project has a valid intent artifact with a prose statement, no resolvable target/referent, no recorded work-unit for that intent, and survey is READY in EvaluationScope::Unscoped.
scenarios.name: go_advances_survey_for_prose_intent_without_a_work_unit
scenarios.then: The completed step is survey with work_unit null, a schema-valid requirements artifact is written through the requirements MCP output tool, and the session advances using the same postcondition-gated artifact recording path as other go ticks.
scenarios.when: The operator runs the single-tick go entry with no work-unit or ticket selector and the agent completes the served current step.

scenarios.criterion: The prose-entry tick reaches the same validated cascade (assess -> select -> context -> output recording -> postcondition-gated commit) as scoped and acquisition ticks; the route is derived from assessed state at the entry boundary, not a new verb or third mode.
scenarios.given: A runa-mcp session is opened without --work-unit and without --ticket for a project where unscoped survey is the next ready protocol from the existing readiness evaluator.
scenarios.name: no_selector_session_serves_the_unscoped_survey_step
scenarios.then: The served context names protocol survey with work_unit null, the advertised output tool set is derived from survey, and advance enforces survey preconditions and postconditions before recording completion.
scenarios.when: A client calls next-protocol-context, produces the required output, and calls advance.

scenarios.criterion: A parity check: the prose-entry interactive tick and the autonomous path produce identical recorded artifacts / execution metadata for the same survey step.
scenarios.given: Two fresh equivalent workspaces contain the same prose intent and use the same deterministic agent behavior to complete survey.
scenarios.name: interactive_and_autonomous_survey_ticks_record_equivalent_completion
scenarios.then: The requirements artifact payloads and the successful execution record for (survey, None) match in the behaviorally relevant fields: input modes, input content hashes, output protocol identity, and work_unit absence.
scenarios.when: One workspace completes survey through the prose-entry interactive go tick and the other through the existing autonomous unscoped execution path.

scenarios.criterion: The go --work-unit and go --ticket contracts and their exit-code semantics are unchanged.
scenarios.given: Existing fixtures cover a recorded scoped work-unit, a cold-start ticket entry, and their blocked/error cases.
scenarios.name: existing_bound_and_ticket_go_entries_keep_their_contracts
scenarios.then: The selected protocols, work_unit values, user-visible errors, and exit outcomes for --work-unit and --ticket are unchanged; the new prose path is selected only when no selector is supplied.
scenarios.when: Those fixtures run after adding the prose-entry path.

scenarios.criterion: No new SessionScope variant is introduced: the prose-entry session evaluates through the existing EvaluationScope::Unscoped path.
scenarios.given: The implementation diff is inspected alongside libagent session readiness, context, and advance authority.
scenarios.name: prose_entry_reuses_unscoped_evaluation_without_a_new_session_scope_variant
scenarios.then: SessionScope still has only Bound and Promised variants, prose entry uses the existing unscoped evaluation mapping and shared select/context/advance code, and no parallel readiness, context-delivery, or transition-authority implementation is added.
scenarios.when: The prose-entry path is traced from CLI go through runa-mcp session opening to SessionState readiness and advance.

title: runa go drives prose intent into unscoped survey
